- **Data Validation Dependencies:** Employee data clean-up and SFTP setup are pending; monthly updates will automate future data accuracy.

- **Anonymity Adjustments Made:** Participant anonymity improved by changing visibility threshold to “3 responses”; enhances trust in survey outcomes.

- **Strong Survey Engagement:** Initial results show ENPS of 68.42; feedback is broken down by engagement channels for better analysis.

- **New Feedback Features Planned:** V2 of feedback tool coming soon; will allow dynamic question branching and sentiment analysis to enhance insights.

- **Upcoming Dashboard Insights:** Beta module set to launch; it consolidates data for high-level analysis and tracks survey trends effectively.

summary.notes:
## **Survey Testing and Launch Preparation**

The survey testing phase showed no technical or user experience issues, and the team is set to finalize before launch.

- **No issues with SMS, WhatsApp delivery, or translations were reported by 40 test participants** as confirmed by **Elaina Skala** and **Karthik Rao** (02:12)  
    - A formal feedback call is scheduled for Friday to capture any remaining concerns.  
    - The survey questions count and time taken will be hidden in the final version, expected to be enabled by the first week of February.  
    - This hiding decision supports cleaner user experience and reduces potential bias from survey length awareness.  
    - Final sign-off depends on feedback from the Friday session; adjustments will follow as needed.

- **Employee data validation and SFTP setup remain the last major launch dependency** per **Karthik** (04:50)  
    - **Rob** is responsible for providing approvals and SFTP details for bulk employee data transfer.  
    - Data clean-up will focus on removing duplicates and blanks to ensure accurate survey participation tracking.  
    - Post-launch, employee data updates will be automated with periodic file deliveries from Rob (monthly or biweekly), eliminating manual updates.  
    - Coordination between Elaina, Christine, Carol, and Rob is critical to finalize timelines and data readiness.

- **Next meeting rescheduled to Tuesday at 9:30 AM Central** to incorporate post-Friday feedback and align on final preparations (06:32)  
    - The updated invite will be confirmed with all stakeholders including Carol.  
    - This timing allows the team to review feedback and make necessary decisions promptly before launch.

## **Survey Anonymity and Reporting Configuration**

Adjustments to anonymity thresholds and reporting features aim to protect participant privacy and enhance data usability.

- **Threshold for team result visibility will change from “3 participants” to “3 responses”** to improve anonymity as explained by **Karthik Rao** (09:48)  
    - This prevents managers from identifying individual responses in small teams where fewer than three employees have submitted feedback.  
    - Elaina confirmed this aligns with their past surveys and privacy standards.  
    - The setting will be enabled after this call but will apply only to the final survey, not the test run.  
    - This change balances transparency with confidentiality to maintain trust in the survey process.

- **Reporting tools have been enhanced with filtering and export options to support leadership and manager-level analysis** (14:00)  
    - Filters cover location, team, and other demographics allowing targeted views across multiple criteria simultaneously.  
    - Export formats include raw data CSV with color coding, PDF, and PPT reports to fit different presentation needs.  
    - Reports show participation rates, engagement scores, and detailed breakouts by business units, tenure, and managers.  
    - The platform allows customization of visible attributes and report order controlled by super admins for tailored views.

## **Survey Metrics and Insights**

Initial test survey results demonstrate strong engagement and provide a foundation for deeper analysis.

- **Test survey participation included 19 respondents across two locations with reporting managers** (07:58)  
    - The current platform logic generates results if teams have more than three participants, but this will change to three actual responses for final surveys.  
    - The Employee Net Promoter Score (ENPS) was **68.42**, indicating very high employee satisfaction and loyalty on a scale from -100 to +100.  
    - Engagement scores and participation data are broken down by channel (email, SMS, QR code), enabling channel performance evaluation.  
    - Leadership and managers will receive summarized reports with actionable insights and detailed question-level scores.

- **Reports include driver-based histograms and question-by-question average scores** (21:56)  
    - Top drivers identified include **Autonomy** and **Involvement**, highlighting key engagement areas.  
    - Color-coded scoring provides quick visual cues on performance across drivers like Recognition (8.8) and Management.  
    - Thematic and correlation analyses will be provided by the people science team to explain what affects ENPS and engagement, guiding improvement priorities.  
    - Custom attributes will be integrated in future surveys to deepen analysis by business-specific factors.

## **Feedback Management and Advanced Features**

The feedback module supports rich qualitative insights and planned enhancements will increase its value.

- **The current feedback tool (V1) allows keyword-based sentiment analysis of open comments** to classify feedback as positive, neutral, or negative (27:43)  
    - A V2 version with enhanced capabilities will launch in the next few weeks to improve usability and insight quality.  
    - Comments flagged as critical by machine learning enable managers to prioritize urgent issues.  
    - Filters cover sentiment, dates, and survey versions to tailor feedback views for more effective follow-up.

- **Dynamic question branching based on low ratings is planned for Q2 but not available for the first launch** (28:24)  
    - This feature would prompt respondents rating below neutral to provide additional comments, improving data richness.  
    - Delays are due to complexities in reporting integration and maintaining consistent data structures.  
    - Elaina noted the feature is under consideration but not yet finalized for rollout.

- **Managers can anonymously initiate conversations with employees based on feedback comments** to address concerns without compromising anonymity (31:52)  
    - This feature sends invitation emails to respondents who can accept or reject chat requests, preserving confidentiality.  
    - The chat interface allows discreet, direct dialogue to improve manager-employee engagement and follow-through.  
    - Super admins and others cannot trace identities, maintaining strong privacy protections.

## **Operational and Technical Readiness**

Process and system enhancements are in place to streamline survey administration and reporting.

- **Access controls allow super admins to set view permissions and reorder report attributes** for different user roles, ensuring relevant data visibility (20:22)  
    - This feature supports segmented data sharing with leadership, HR, and managers while protecting sensitive info.  
    - The platform's flexible filtering system covers both default and upcoming custom attributes, enabling detailed segmentation.

- **The Insights module, currently in beta, will provide high-level analysis dashboards in coming months** (24:48)  
    - This will reduce reliance on external data exports by consolidating analytic views within the platform.  
    - It supports cross-survey comparisons and can track trends over multiple survey waves planned for the year.  
    - Availability is scheduled before the second quarter to align with the second survey cycle.

- **IP and domain whitelisting for survey access is pending but prioritized by Elaina’s team** to ensure smooth rollout across all clubs (35:15)  
    - Finalizing the list of target clubs for the first survey will help focus this effort.  
    - This step is critical to avoid access issues and ensure broad participation.

- **Report customization requests require a lead time of about seven working days** to deliver tailored analysis and presentations for leadership (33:00)  
    - Raw data is available immediately after survey submissions for real-time monitoring.  
    - Custom reports will include thematic analysis, correlation studies, and branded formatting per client needs.

- **The next survey wave and reporting cadence are planned quarterly with incremental feature releases** to enhance data quality and actionability over time.

summary.overview:
- **Survey Delivery:** Test survey emails delivered successfully; one non-delivery issue being investigated for accurate data collection.  
- **Data Migration Complete:** Historical employee data migration finished; engineering team finalizing script for integrating new hire survey responses.  
- **Dashboard Insights:** Culture Monkey dashboards show participation rates, bounce rates, and engagement scores; 100% response completion rate observed.  
- **Anonymity Strategy:** Surveys will remain anonymous to encourage honest feedback; new hire surveys may identify individual responses for immediate action.  
- **Next Meeting:** Follow-up planned next week to review script status and ensure alignment for upcoming organizational survey launch.  
- **Stakeholder Preparation:** Ongoing support offered for platform questions; inclusion of team members ensures readiness for broader survey rollout.

summary.notes:
## **Survey Delivery and Participation**

The team confirmed **successful delivery of the test survey emails with one reported non-delivery incident** that Yugi will investigate by reviewing delivery logs to ensure full receipt (02:30).  
- Philip identified the participant who did not receive the email and confirmed no other delivery issues were reported.  
- Yugi will check email delivery and spam filters to resolve this potential one-off issue and update post-call.  
- Katie emphasized the importance of confirming full email receipt to maintain engagement accuracy.  
- This step is critical to ensure reliable data collection in upcoming organizational surveys.

## **Data Integration and Historical Migration**

The historical employee data migration is **complete on KAG’s side, with the engineering team finishing a script to load new hire survey responses into Culture Monkey** for full data integration (06:07).  
- Yugi explained that the script captures survey responses and will be tested on sample data before full migration.  
- Katie and Philip confirmed Stephen and Sarah’s work on file uploads is finished, freeing them from the project (06:13).  
- This migration enables visibility of historical data alongside new responses, improving trend analysis.  
- The timeline for testing and full load will be shared shortly to facilitate further review.

## **Dashboard Reporting and Survey Analytics**

The Culture Monkey dashboards now provide **comprehensive survey insights including participation rates, bounce rates, ENPS scores, and detailed demographic heat maps** to guide targeted action (07:11).  
- Yugi demonstrated the test survey dashboard showing **100% response completion rate and an ENPS of 28.57**, indicating a generally positive employee sentiment.  
- Participation trends highlight days with highest responses, enabling strategic timing for reminders.  
- Heat maps display scores by teams and locations with an anonymity threshold of **minimum 3 participants per cohort** to protect privacy.  
- The system allows drill down into drivers behind scores and question-level breakdowns to pinpoint specific issues.  
- Export features were confirmed for offline analysis using Excel, supporting manual deep dives if needed.

## **Anonymity and Data Privacy Strategy**

Philip clarified that **organizational engagement surveys will be fully anonymous to encourage honest feedback**, while **new hire surveys may reveal individual responses for early issue identification** (22:42).  
- This dual approach balances confidentiality with actionability to address problems promptly.  
- Katie acknowledged that anonymity was a key vendor selection criterion to build trust and encourage participation.  
- The platform supports both anonymized aggregate reporting and confidential exports for super admins as needed.  
- This strategy aims to drive higher survey completion rates and accurate data for meaningful interventions.

## **Next Steps and Follow-Up Planning**

Katie planned a follow-up meeting next week to review the historical data script status and wrap up remaining items, targeting a full system walkthrough before the organizational survey launch (24:26).  
- Philip requested advance notice of any timeline delays to adjust schedules accordingly.  
- Yugi offered ongoing support for any platform questions as Philip explores the system further.  
- Philip intends to include Carly in the next call to familiarize her with the platform.  
- This phased approach ensures all stakeholders are prepared and aligned ahead of the broader survey rollout.

summary.overview:
- **Candidate Lifecycle Analytics:** Building a unified platform for candidate analytics with custom filters for focused insights and usability.  
- **Access Controls:** Hiring managers will have distinct login access, limiting exposure to unrelated employee data for enhanced security.  
- **Multi-Language Support:** Platform to include multilingual capabilities for surveys and customizable time zones, boosting global engagement.  
- **Logic Branching:** Implementing routing for surveys based on interview stages, ensuring data relevance and accuracy for hired candidates.  
- **API Testing:** Validating data with 10-20 candidate records to ensure smooth integration and accurate data flow for analytics.  
- **Meeting Coordination:** Scheduling focused meetings to prevent conflicts and maintain efficient decision-making for project progress.
summary.action_items:
**Karthik Rao**

Test candidate data upload process and verify mandatory fields enforcement, including data validation pop-ups (28:00)
Confirm translation support status for terms and conditions and button labels with product team and communicate limitations (40:00)
Resolve hiring manager login access and filter responsiveness issues to improve user experience (41:00)
Implement multilingual support and time zone customization in CS panel configurations (56:00)
Finalize logic branching rules for candidate survey routing based on interview/assessment stages (01:05:00)
Coordinate API testing with Yahoo’s team, obtain test candidate data sets for logic validation (01:12:00)

**Scott**
Act as primary point of contact for candidate module post initial calls, maintain visibility on candidate-specific issues and follow up with engagement team (01:14:00)
summary.notes:
## **Candidate Lifecycle Analytics and Reporting**

The team decided to build a **single view candidate lifecycle analytics** platform with custom filters and support for detailed engagement metrics (14:07).

- **Karthik Rao confirmed** the plan to support a single view report for candidate lifecycle with customizable filters beyond default attributes to accommodate client-specific needs.
- The candidate lifecycle analytics will be separate from employee dashboards and accessed via a dedicated candidate switcher button for clarity and usability.
- The platform will include detailed question-wise breakdowns for engagement surveys, supporting multi-select and rating question types within the candidate context.
- This approach aligns with Yahoo’s request to provide lifecycle data specific to candidates without mixing it with employee data, ensuring focused analytics for hiring managers.

## **Access Controls and Role-Based Reporting**

A clear separation of access roles will be implemented to allow **hiring managers distinct login and reporting capabilities** for candidate data, enhancing data security and usability (41:50).

- Hiring managers will receive tailored access to job posting and candidate lifecycle reports without exposure to unrelated employee data.
- The system will enable role-specific views where hiring managers can see relevant candidate details like team, office location, and contact information but not full employee records.
- Karthik highlighted existing UI responsiveness issues in manager profile views, which will be fixed to improve user experience and ensure complete access to hiring manager details.
- This role-based access will reduce data clutter and improve decision-making by providing focused information to the right users.

## **Multi-Language and Customization Enhancements**

The platform will support **multilingual engagement and candidate surveys**, including translation options and time zone customizations to meet global client needs (56:47).

- Multilingual support will be medium priority with options to enable language selection in the CS panel, allowing clients to launch surveys in different languages via channels like Slack.
- Time zone settings will be customizable per account to ensure accurate scheduling and reporting across regions.
- Translation capabilities for terms and conditions pages will be limited to hiding options rather than editable text, as per current platform constraints.
- These enhancements aim to improve candidate engagement across diverse geographies and increase response rates by providing localized experiences.

## **Logic Branching and Survey Stage Routing**

The team will implement **logic branching for candidate surveys**, routing candidates to stage-specific surveys based on interview and assessment status, improving data relevance (01:04:57).

- Karthik outlined a plan to define **five key conditions** related to interview stages and assessment results, mapping each to a unique survey.
- This routing will automate pushing candidates to the correct survey stage, reducing manual errors and increasing data accuracy.
- The team will confirm final conditions with the client before full implementation to ensure alignment with hiring workflows.
- This feature supports more granular insights into candidate experiences at each hiring stage, enhancing targeted improvements.

## **API Integration and Data Validation**

Yahoo will be asked to **test their API with 10 to 20 candidate records** to validate data attributes and values before building logic, ensuring smooth integration and accurate data flow (01:13:45).

- The team plans to start with API testing and configuration sharing, aiming to finalize categories and logic conditions quickly.
- Scott will take responsibility as the candidate POC for ongoing communication and follow-up during integration.
- This phased approach aims to minimize integration issues and speed up delivery by iterating on real data samples.
- Confirming attribute mappings and data consistency upfront reduces risk and supports a reliable candidate lifecycle analytics rollout.

## **Meeting Cadence and Coordination**

Karthik emphasized the importance of **coordinating meeting times and avoiding conflicts** with other critical calls to maintain efficient progress (01:16:51).

- He noted existing calendar blocks from 3 to 4 PM and suggested avoiding scheduling during these to prevent disruptions.
- The team agreed to keep the Yahoo call within an hour, with Scott handling follow-ups to keep the process lean.
- This disciplined approach to scheduling supports timely decision-making and keeps all stakeholders aligned on priorities.
- Maintaining focused, well-timed calls reduces delays and supports faster delivery of candidate lifecycle features.
